rationale: Hard pass for Archery Clash. The dark noir crime-scene palette (#1a1a2e/brown) and investigative mystery framing clash directly with the game's bright blue/gold competitive PvP energy and stylized female-archer fantasy — there's no narrative bridge to aiming, dueling, or gear collection. Audience overlap is also weak: curiosity/whodunit viewers skew toward narrative puzzle and sim players, not the young-male PvP shooter crowd. Only worth testing if the team can radically reskin the hook (e.g., archer investigating the scene, then drawing a bow), otherwise skip.
